note: Grouped catalog products for Encore Bio Labs. slug and image reuse the real values already present in catalogProducts/assets in src/data/products.ts — no new slugs or filenames were invented since matching entries and image assets already exist. category matches the real `category` field already used in catalogProducts (not the 7-way catalog-page filter grouping used elsewhere). One entry per product; multi-strength products are grouped under a single entry with a variations array, per the no-duplication rule.

products.title: Retatrutide
products.shortDescription: Studied as a triple agonist that engages GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously. Research in this area focuses on incretin-pathway signaling and metabolic marker response, not individual outcomes.

products.title: Tesamorelin
products.shortDescription: A synthetic GHRH analog studied for its effect on GH-axis signaling and downstream IGF-1 response. Commonly reviewed in visceral-adiposity and metabolic-marker research models.

products.title: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin
products.shortDescription: A combination entry pairing a GHRH analog with a ghrelin-receptor secretagogue for dual-pathway GH-axis research. Grouped as one product since the two are almost always studied together.

products.title: MOTS-C
products.shortDescription: A mitochondria-derived peptide studied for its connection to AMPK-linked energy sensing and metabolic-stress research. Distinct in mechanism from the incretin- and GHRH-based compounds in this category.

products.title: Wolverine Stack
products.shortDescription: A packaged combination of BPC-157 and TB-500 research themes for studies that look at both compounds together. Organized as one kit rather than two separate research entries.

products.title: BPC-157
products.shortDescription: A gastric-derived peptide fragment studied in preclinical models of repair signaling, angiogenesis, and gut-barrier biology. One of the most frequently referenced compounds in tissue-repair research literature.

products.title: TB-500
products.shortDescription: A synthetic peptide related to thymosin beta-4, studied for its role in actin regulation and cell-migration research. Often reviewed alongside BPC-157 in recovery-focused study design.

products.title: KLOW
products.shortDescription: A research-support kit entry focused on handling, documentation, and companion-component logistics rather than a single biological pathway. Built to keep supporting materials organized for a research order.

products.title: GHK-Cu
products.shortDescription: A copper tripeptide complex studied for extracellular-matrix remodeling, collagen signaling, and wound-response research. Broader in research scope than its follicle-focused counterpart, AHK-Cu.

products.title: AHK-Cu
products.shortDescription: A related copper peptide studied more specifically in follicle and dermal-signaling research contexts. Shares copper-peptide biology with GHK-Cu but with a narrower research focus.

products.title: IGF-1 LR3
products.shortDescription: A long-acting IGF-1 analog studied for receptor-binding characteristics and downstream cellular-growth signaling. Reviewed in performance-oriented and growth-pathway research contexts.

products.title: Cerebrolysin
products.shortDescription: A neurotrophic peptide mixture studied for neuronal-survival models and synaptic-plasticity research. Reviewed as a compound mixture rather than a single-sequence peptide.

products.title: Semax
products.shortDescription: A synthetic ACTH-fragment analog studied for neuropeptide signaling and BDNF-related expression. Frequently reviewed alongside Selank in cognitive-research planning.

products.title: Selank
products.shortDescription: A synthetic tuftsin analog studied for stress-response and neuroimmune signaling research. Distinguished from Semax by its serotonin-adjacent research angle.

products.title: NAD+
products.shortDescription: A central cellular cofactor studied for its role in redox biology, mitochondrial energy metabolism, and sirtuin-linked aging research. One of the most foundational compounds in longevity research literature.

products.title: Glutathione
products.shortDescription: An endogenous tripeptide antioxidant studied for redox balance and detoxification enzyme systems. Frequently referenced as the primary intracellular antioxidant buffer in cellular-defense research.

products.title: Epithalon
products.shortDescription: A synthetic tetrapeptide studied in telomere-associated, pineal-peptide, and circadian-rhythm research contexts. Positioned in aging-biology literature without any lifespan or outcome claims.

products.title: SS-31
products.shortDescription: A mitochondria-targeted peptide studied for its interaction with cardiolipin and inner-membrane stability. One of the most mechanism-specific entries in mitochondrial research literature.

products.title: Thymosin Alpha-1
products.shortDescription: An immune-signaling peptide studied for T-cell function and cellular-defense research relevance. Included in longevity research for its overlap with immune-resilience biology.

products.title: DSIP
products.shortDescription: A neuropeptide studied for sleep-architecture models and neuroendocrine signaling research. Reviewed in the context of stress-response and rest-related biology, not sleep treatment.

products.title: Kisspeptin
products.shortDescription: A neuropeptide studied for reproductive-axis signaling and GnRH pulse regulation. Sits upstream in the hormonal-axis research picture relative to downstream gonadotropins like HCG.

products.title: HCG
products.shortDescription: A glycoprotein hormone studied for LH/CG receptor signaling and gonadal steroidogenesis research. Acts further downstream in the reproductive axis than upstream regulators like Kisspeptin.

products.title: HGH 191AA
products.shortDescription: The 191-amino-acid human growth hormone sequence, studied for GH receptor signaling and IGF-1 axis response. Reviewed in research contexts around growth-hormone-axis biology.

products.title: PT-141
products.shortDescription: A melanocortin receptor agonist studied for central nervous system signaling relevant to sexual-wellness research models. Commonly associated with bremelanotide in research literature.
